data.title: Brainstorm skill asks user questions that could be answered by investigating the project
data.description:
## What happened

During brainstorm for epic dso-v558 (Figma integration), the skill asked the user what form proposed designs take (ASCII wireframes, screenshots, design specs, etc.) — presenting 4 options for something that is fully discoverable by reading the existing design-wireframe skill.

The user had to redirect: "Use a sub-agent to research the project. You're already aware of our design-wireframe skill and other design/ui skills. Investigate what we do now with UI changes."

## Why it happened

The brainstorm skill's Phase 1 Step 2 ("Tell Me More" loop) is biased toward asking questions rather than investigating. The skill loads PRD and design-notes.md in Step 1, but does NOT investigate existing skills, workflows, or codebase structure before asking questions. When the feature being brainstormed relates to an existing capability (like the design workflow), the skill should exhaust what it can learn from the codebase before asking the user.

## Rationalization at the time

The agent reasoned: "The user described wanting to send proposed designs to Figma — I should ask what form those designs take so I can understand the translation needed." This is a reasonable question in isolation, but the agent already knew about /dso:design-wireframe, /dso:design-review, /dso:ui-discover, and /dso:design-onboarding from CLAUDE.md. It could have dispatched a sub-agent to read those skills and synthesized what the current design artifacts look like BEFORE asking the user.

## Fix needed

The brainstorm skill's Phase 1 should include explicit guidance: when the feature being brainstormed relates to existing project capabilities, dispatch an Explore sub-agent to investigate those capabilities BEFORE entering the question loop. Only ask the user questions that cannot be answered by reading the project.

This aligns with the using-lockpick guidance: "Silent Investigation: Before asking anything, read code, tickets, git history, CLAUDE.md, and memory to gather context that already exists."
